878:     }
879: 
880:     lockHeld = await acquireBootstrapLock();
881:     if (lockHeld) {
882:       // Auto-migrate DB from the former shared standalone location back to skill-local.
883:       // The former DB is preserved as a backup (copy, not move).
884:       // This block now runs ONLY for the single bootstrap-lock winner (lockHeld), AFTER the lock
885:       // is held and BEFORE the DB is opened by the spawned child (launchServer, below). Previously
886:       // it ran in the main IIFE before any lock, so two concurrent launchers could both pass the
887:       // !exists(target) guard and both copy — a lagging copier truncate-overwriting the now-LIVE
888:       // target DB out from under the winner's daemon (SQLite corruption). Gating on lockHeld makes
889:       // only one launcher migrate; the COPYFILE_EXCL + pre-copy re-check below is the belt-and-
890:       // suspenders guarantee that NO launcher ever overwrites an existing (possibly live) target DB.
891:       // Never auto-seed/migrate into an explicit SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_DB_DIR override
892:       //   (the operator chose that target); migrate only into the resolved default.
893:       // Never copy a former DB that a live legacy owner could still be
894:       //   writing — probe BOTH the former PID lease and the former owner lease for liveness first.
895:       //   The former owner lease (.code-graph-owner.json) is intentionally NOT copied forward.
896:       const formerSharedDbDir = path.join(opencodeDir, '.spec-kit', 'code-graph', 'database');
897:       const migrationTarget = resolvedDbDir();
898:       if (!process.env.SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_DB_DIR
899:           && !exists(path.join(migrationTarget, 'code-graph.sqlite'))
900:           && exists(path.join(formerSharedDbDir, 'code-graph.sqlite'))
901:           && !formerLocationOwnerLive(formerSharedDbDir)) {
902:         fs.mkdirSync(migrationTarget, { recursive: true, mode: 0o700 });
903:         const dbFiles = [
904:           'code-graph.sqlite',
905:           'code-graph.sqlite-shm',
906:           'code-graph.sqlite-wal',
907:           '.code-graph-readiness.json',
908:           '.mk-code-index-launcher.json',
909:         ];
910:         // Re-check the sqlite target immediately before copying so a launcher that
911:         // raced past the outer guard (e.g. a concurrent migrator that just finished) does not
912:         // clobber the freshly-migrated DB. EEXIST from COPYFILE_EXCL means another launcher
913:         // already migrated that file -> treat the target as authoritative and stop migrating.
914:         let migrated = false;
915:         if (!exists(path.join(migrationTarget, 'code-graph.sqlite'))) {
916:           try {
917:             for (const file of dbFiles) {
918:               const src = path.join(formerSharedDbDir, file);
919:               const dst = path.join(migrationTarget, file);
920:               if (exists(src)) {
921:                 // COPYFILE_EXCL makes the copy fail with EEXIST rather than truncate-
922:                 // overwrite an existing (possibly live) target file.
923:                 fs.copyFileSync(src, dst, fs.constants.COPYFILE_EXCL);
924:               }
925:             }
926:             migrated = true;
927:           } catch (copyError) {
928:             if (copyError.code === 'EEXIST') {
929:               // Another launcher already migrated into this target; the existing target
930:               // is authoritative. Do NOT overwrite it.
931:               log('migration skipped: target already present (EEXIST); treating existing target as authoritative');
932:             } else {
933:               throw copyError;
934:             }
935:           }
936:         }
937:         if (migrated) {
938:           process.stderr.write(
939:             `[mk-code-index-launcher] migrated DB from ${rel(formerSharedDbDir)} to ${rel(migrationTarget)} (former location preserved)\n`
940:           );
941:         }
942:       }
943: 
944:       buildIfNeeded(actions);
945:       log(`ready: ${JSON.stringify({ start: started, end: now(), actions, server: rel(path.join(kitDir, 'mcp_server', 'dist', 'index.js')) })}`);
946:     }
947: 
948:     writeLeaseFile();
949:     const reprobe = readLeaseFile();
950:     if (!reprobe || reprobe.pid !== process.pid) {
951:       const startedAt = reprobe?.startedAt ?? new Date(0).toISOString();
952:       process.stdout.write(`LEASE_HELD_BY:${reprobe ? reprobe.pid : 'unknown'} startedAt=${startedAt}\n`);
953:       process.exit(0);
954:     }
955:     launchServer();
956:   } catch (error) {
957:     log(`failed: ${JSON.stringify({ start: started, end: now(), actions, error: error.message })}`);
958:     log(error.stack || error.message);
959:     process.exit(1);
960:   } finally {
961:     if (lockHeld) {
962:       fs.rmSync(lockDir, { recursive: true, force: true });
963:     }
964:   }

30:   return clampProbeTimeoutMs(parsePositiveInteger(
31:     (options.env ?? process.env).SPECKIT_PROBE_TIMEOUT_MS,

69: function toConnectionOptions(socketPath) {
70:   if (!socketPath.startsWith('tcp://')) {
71:     return socketPath;
72:   }
73:   const url = new URL(socketPath);
74:   return {
75:     host: url.hostname,
76:     port: Number.parseInt(url.port, 10),
77:   };

80: function bridgeStdioToSocket(socketPath, options = {}) {
81:   const input = options.stdin ?? process.stdin;
82:   const output = options.stdout ?? process.stdout;
83:   const exit = options.exit ?? ((code) => process.exit(code));
84:   const socket = net.createConnection(toConnectionOptions(socketPath));
85:   let closed = false;
86: 
87:   const closeOnce = (code = 0) => {
88:     if (closed) return;
89:     closed = true;
90:     input.unpipe(socket);
91:     socket.unpipe(output);
92:     socket.destroy();
93:     exit(code);
94:   };
95: 
96:   socket.once('connect', () => {
97:     options.onConnect?.(socket);
98:     input.pipe(socket);
99:     socket.pipe(output);
100:   });
101: 
102:   socket.once('error', (error) => {
103:     options.onError?.(error);
104:     closeOnce(0);
105:   });
106: 
107:   socket.once('close', () => {
108:     closeOnce(0);
109:   });
110: 
111:   input.once('end', () => {
112:     socket.end();
113:   });
114:   input.once('close', () => {
115:     closeOnce(0);
116:   });
117:   output.once('error', () => {
118:     closeOnce(0);
119:   });

121:   return socket;
122: }

124: function probeDaemon(socketPath, options = {}) {
125:   const timeoutMs = resolveProbeTimeoutMs(options);
126:   const connect = options.connect ?? ((connectionOptions) => net.createConnection(connectionOptions));
127:   // deepProbe=true REQUIRES a JSON-RPC initialize reply to call the daemon alive (detects a hung daemon
128:   // that accepts the connection but never responds). Without it, a bare socket 'connect' counts as alive
129:   // (connect-ok) — only safe for callers that genuinely need connection-liveness, NOT for the reap/bridge
130:   // decision (which always passes deepProbe:true). See maybeBridgeLeaseHolder.
131:   const deepProbe = options.deepProbe === true;
132:   const id = nextProbeId++;
133:   const request = `${JSON.stringify({
134:     jsonrpc: '2.0',
135:     id,
136:     method: 'initialize',
137:     params: {
138:       protocolVersion: JSON_RPC_PROTOCOL_VERSION,
139:       capabilities: {},
140:       clientInfo: { name: 'liveness-probe', version: '0' },
141:     },
142:   })}\n`;
143: 
144:   return new Promise((resolve) => {
145:     let socket;
146:     let settled = false;
147:     let buffer = '';
148:     let timer;
149:     // Hold a multibyte char split across chunk boundaries until complete, so a probe reply
150:     // carrying CJK/emoji (e.g. serverInfo strings) is not corrupted to U+FFFD mid-parse.
151:     const decoder = new StringDecoder('utf8');
152: 
153:     const finish = (status, reason) => {
154:       if (settled) return;
155:       settled = true;
156:       if (timer) clearTimeout(timer);
157:       if (socket) socket.destroy();
158:       resolve({ status, reason });
159:     };
160: 
161:     try {
162:       socket = connect(toConnectionOptions(socketPath));
163:     } catch (error) {
164:       finish('dead', error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'connect-threw');
165:       return;
166:     }
167: 
168:     timer = setTimeout(() => finish('dead', 'timeout'), timeoutMs);
169:     timer.unref?.();
170: 
171:     socket.once('connect', () => {
172:       if (!deepProbe) {
173:         finish('alive', 'connect-ok');
174:         return;
175:       }
176:       socket.write(request);
177:     });
178:     socket.on('data', (chunk) => {
179:       buffer += Buffer.isBuffer(chunk) ? decoder.write(chunk) : String(chunk ?? '');
180:       let newlineIndex = buffer.indexOf('\n');
181:       while (newlineIndex !== -1) {
182:         const line = buffer.slice(0, newlineIndex).trim();
183:         buffer = buffer.slice(newlineIndex + 1);
184:         newlineIndex = buffer.indexOf('\n');
185:         if (!line) continue;
186:         try {
187:           const parsed = JSON.parse(line);
188:           if (
189:             parsed &&
190:             parsed.jsonrpc === '2.0' &&
191:             parsed.id === id &&
192:             (Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(parsed, 'result') ||
193:               Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(parsed, 'error'))
194:           ) {
195:             finish('alive', 'json-rpc-reply');
196:             return;
197:           }
198:         } catch {
199:           // Ignore malformed or unrelated frames; the bounded probe decides liveness.
200:         }
201:       }
202:     });
203:     socket.once('error', (error) => {
204:       finish('dead', error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'socket-error');
205:     });
206:     socket.once('close', () => {
207:       finish('dead', 'closed-before-reply');
208:     });
209:   });
210: }
211: 
212: function parseHttpJsonResponse(buffer) {
213:   const raw = buffer.toString('utf8');
214:   const headerEnd = raw.indexOf('\r\n\r\n');
215:   if (headerEnd === -1) return null;
216:   const statusLine = raw.slice(0, raw.indexOf('\r\n'));
217:   const statusMatch = statusLine.match(/^HTTP\/\d(?:\.\d)?\s+(\d+)/);
218:   if (!statusMatch) return null;
219:   const statusCode = Number.parseInt(statusMatch[1], 10);
220:   const bodyRaw = raw.slice(headerEnd + 4).trim();
221:   let body = null;
222:   try {
223:     body = bodyRaw ? JSON.parse(bodyRaw) : null;
224:   } catch {
225:     return { statusCode, body: null };
226:   }
227:   return { statusCode, body };
228: }
229: 
230: function probeModelServer(socketPath, options = {}) {
231:   const timeoutMs = resolveProbeTimeoutMs(options);
232:   const loadingMaxMs = Number.isFinite(options.loadingMaxMs) && options.loadingMaxMs > 0
233:     ? options.loadingMaxMs
234:     : parsePositiveInteger(
235:       (options.env ?? process.env).SPECKIT_HF_MODEL_SERVER_LOADING_MAX_MS,
236:       DEFAULT_MODEL_SERVER_LOADING_MAX_MS,
237:     );
238:   const nowMs = typeof options.nowMs === 'function' ? options.nowMs : () => Date.now();
239:   const connect = options.connect ?? ((connectionOptions) => net.createConnection(connectionOptions));
240:   // Mark this as the launcher's internal liveness probe so a cold-state demand listener can answer
241:   // without spawning a model server. Genuine embed consumers do not send this header, so their
242:   // wake-on-demand path is unchanged.
243:   const request = `GET ${MODEL_SERVER_HEALTH_PATH} HTTP/1.1\r\nHost: localhost\r\nAccept: application/json\r\nX-Speckit-Probe: liveness\r\nConnection: close\r\n\r\n`;
244: 
245:   return new Promise((resolve) => {
246:     let socket;
247:     let settled = false;
248:     let chunks = [];
249:     let timer;
250: 
251:     // Carry the parsed health body so callers (the idle-eviction tick) can read
252:     // lastSuccessfulEmbedAt / inFlight. The 'alive' branches below pass parsed.body as the 3rd arg.
253:     const finish = (status, reason, health) => {
254:       if (settled) return;
255:       settled = true;
256:       if (timer) clearTimeout(timer);
257:       if (socket) socket.destroy();
258:       resolve({ status, reason, health });
259:     };
260: 
261:     try {
262:       socket = connect(toConnectionOptions(socketPath));
263:     } catch (error) {
264:       finish('dead', error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'connect-threw');
265:       return;
266:     }
267: 
268:     timer = setTimeout(() => finish('dead', 'timeout'), timeoutMs);
269:     timer.unref?.();
270: 
271:     socket.once('connect', () => {
272:       socket.write(request);
273:     });
274:     socket.on('data', (chunk) => {
275:       chunks.push(Buffer.isBuffer(chunk) ? chunk : Buffer.from(String(chunk ?? '')));
276:     });
277:     socket.once('end', () => {
278:       const parsed = parseHttpJsonResponse(Buffer.concat(chunks));
279:       const state = parsed && parsed.body && typeof parsed.body === 'object'
280:         ? parsed.body.state
281:         : null;
282:       if (parsed && parsed.statusCode >= 200 && parsed.statusCode < 300 && state === 'ready') {
283:         finish('alive', `health-${state}`, parsed.body);
284:         return;
285:       }
286:       if (parsed && parsed.statusCode >= 200 && parsed.statusCode < 300 && state === 'loading') {
287:         const loadStartedAt = parsed.body.loadStartedAt;
288:         const loadProgressAt = parsed.body.loadProgressAt;
289:         // The loading budget is per model-load attempt; device fallback re-stamps loadProgressAt.
290:         const loadingMarker = Number.isFinite(loadProgressAt) && loadProgressAt > 0
291:           ? loadProgressAt
292:           : loadStartedAt;
293:         if (Number.isFinite(loadingMarker) && loadingMarker > 0 && nowMs() - loadingMarker > loadingMaxMs) {
294:           finish('dead', 'loading-wedged');
295:           return;
296:         }
297:         finish('alive', 'health-loading', parsed.body);
298:         return;
299:       }
300:       finish('dead', state === 'error' ? 'health-error' : 'health-not-ready');
301:     });
302:     socket.once('error', (error) => {
303:       finish('dead', error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'socket-error');
304:     });
305:     socket.once('close', () => {
306:       if (!settled && chunks.length === 0) finish('dead', 'closed-before-reply');
307:     });
308:   });
309: }

311: async function maybeBridgeLeaseHolder(options) {
312:   const {
313:     serviceName,
314:     leaseResult,
315:     loggerPrefix,
316:     dbDir,
317:     legacyReport,
318:     probeTimeoutMs,
319:     connect,
320:     bridge,
321:   } = options;
322:   const startedAt = leaseResult.startedAt ?? new Date(0).toISOString();
323:   const legacyMarker = leaseResult.legacyPath ? ' (legacy path)' : '';
324:   const ownerPid = leaseResult.ownerPid;
325:   const writeLeaseHeld = (suffix = '') => {
326:     process.stdout.write(`LEASE_HELD_BY:${ownerPid} startedAt=${startedAt}${legacyMarker}${suffix}\n`);
327:   };
328: 
329:   if (process.env.SPECKIT_LAUNCHER_BRIDGE_DISABLED === '1') {
330:     if (legacyReport) {
331:       legacyReport(leaseResult);
332:       return { action: 'report', reason: 'bridge-disabled' };
333:     }
334:     writeLeaseHeld();
335:     return { action: 'report', reason: 'bridge-disabled' };
336:   }
337: 
338:   // Prefer the path the lease owner actually recorded over recomputing one from env. Under a
339:   // divergent SPECKIT_IPC_SOCKET_DIR (e.g. a secondary launcher in a different worktree env), the
340:   // recomputed path can miss the live socket and false-report 'no-bridge-socket'. Only trust the
341:   // stored path when it still exists on disk; tcp:// endpoints bypass the existence check. Fall back
342:   // to recomputation for legacy leases that predate socketPath and for the other launchers whose
343:   // leases never carry it.
344:   const storedSocketPath = leaseResult.socketPath;
345:   const usableStoredSocketPath = typeof storedSocketPath === 'string'
346:     && storedSocketPath.length > 0
347:     && (storedSocketPath.startsWith('tcp://') || fs.existsSync(storedSocketPath))
348:     ? storedSocketPath
349:     : null;
350:   const socketPath = usableStoredSocketPath ?? getIpcSocketPath(serviceName, { dbDir });
351:   if (!socketPath.startsWith('tcp://') && !fs.existsSync(socketPath)) {
352:     writeLeaseHeld(' (no-bridge-socket)');
353:     return { action: 'report', reason: 'no-bridge-socket', socketPath };
354:   }
355: 
356:   // deepProbe: REQUIRE a JSON-RPC reply, not just a socket accept. The reap/bridge decision must
357:   // detect a HUNG daemon (event loop wedged / deadlocked — it still accepts the UDS connection but
358:   // never services requests). A connect-ok-only probe would bridge a client to the wedged daemon and
359:   // hang it forever, never respawning. The raised probe timeout (default 5000ms, < the 7000ms launcher
360:   // grace) already prevents false-reaping a busy-but-responsive daemon mid-FTS-merge, so deep probing
361:   // does not regress that.
362:   const probe = await probeDaemon(socketPath, { timeoutMs: probeTimeoutMs, connect, deepProbe: true });
363:   if (probe.status !== 'alive') {
364:     process.stderr.write(`[${loggerPrefix}] lease holder pid=${ownerPid} socket=${socketPath} failed liveness probe: ${probe.reason}\n`);
365:     return { action: 'respawn', reason: probe.reason, socketPath };
366:   }
367: 
368:   process.stderr.write(`[${loggerPrefix}] bridging to lease holder pid=${ownerPid} socket=${socketPath}\n`);
369:   const bridgeToSocket = bridge ?? bridgeStdioToSocket;
370:   // Await so an injected reconnecting bridge (whose start() resolves after the first
371:   // attach) is fully wired before returning. The raw bridge returns a socket, not a
372:   // promise, so Promise.resolve keeps the original fire-and-forget timing for it.
373:   await Promise.resolve(bridgeToSocket(socketPath, {
374:     onError: () => {
375:       writeLeaseHeld(' (bridge-refused)');
376:     },
377:   }));
378:   return { action: 'bridge', socketPath };
379: }
380: 
381: module.exports = {
382:   DEFAULT_MODEL_SERVER_LOADING_MAX_MS,
383:   bridgeStdioToSocket,
384:   getIpcSocketPath,
385:   maybeBridgeLeaseHolder,
386:   probeDaemon,
387:   probeModelServer,
388:   toConnectionOptions,
389: };

(End of file - total 389 lines)

209:     const result = await probe(socketPath, { connect, deepProbe: true });
210:     if (result.status === 'alive') return result;
211:     if (attempt + 1 >= maxAttempts) return result;
212:     const delayMs = PROBE_BACKOFF_MS[Math.min(attempt, PROBE_BACKOFF_MS.length - 1)];
213:     log(`daemon socket not ready (${result.reason}); retrying in ${delayMs}ms`);
214:     await sleep(delayMs);
215:   }
216:   return { status: 'dead', reason: 'reattach-attempts-exhausted' };

246:       return;
247:     }

251:       socket.off?.('error', onError);
252:     };
253:     const onConnect = () => {
254:       cleanup();
255:       resolve(socket);
256:     };
257:     const onError = (error) => {
258:       cleanup();
259:       socket.destroy?.();
260:       reject(error);
261:     };

263:     socket.once('connect', onConnect);
264:     socket.once('error', onError);

268: function internalHandshake(socket, initializeFrame) {
269:   if (!initializeFrame) return Promise.resolve({ residual: '', protocolVersion: undefined, handshakeObserved: false, initializeResponse: null });

271:   const initialize = parseFrame(initializeFrame);
272:   if (!initialize || !hasRequestId(initialize)) {
273:     return Promise.resolve({ residual: '', protocolVersion: undefined, handshakeObserved: false, initializeResponse: null });
274:   }
275: 
276:   return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
277:     let buffer = '';
278:     let residual = '';
279:     let negotiatedVersion;
280:     let initializeResponse = null;
281:     // Hold a multibyte char split across chunk boundaries until complete, so a CJK/emoji
282:     // payload in the residual (post-handshake) stream is not corrupted to U+FFFD.
283:     const decoder = new StringDecoder('utf8');
284:     const timer = setTimeout(() => {
285:       cleanup();
286:       reject(new Error('timed out waiting for internal initialize'));
287:     }, INTERNAL_HANDSHAKE_TIMEOUT_MS);
288:     timer.unref?.();
289:     const cleanup = () => {
290:       clearTimeout(timer);
291:       socket.off?.('data', onData);
292:       socket.off?.('error', onError);
293:       socket.off?.('close', onClose);
294:     };
295:     const onData = (chunk) => {
296:       buffer += Buffer.isBuffer(chunk) ? decoder.write(chunk) : String(chunk ?? '');
297:       let initialized = false;
298:       let newlineIndex = buffer.indexOf('\n');
299:       while (newlineIndex !== -1) {
300:         const frame = buffer.slice(0, newlineIndex);
301:         buffer = buffer.slice(newlineIndex + 1);
302:         newlineIndex = buffer.indexOf('\n');
303:         if (frame.trim().length === 0) continue;
304:         const parsed = parseFrame(frame);
305:         if (parsed && isResponseFrame(parsed) && parsed.id === initialize.id) {
306:           initialized = true;
307:           initializeResponse = frame;
308:           negotiatedVersion = parsed.result && typeof parsed.result === 'object'
309:             ? parsed.result.protocolVersion
310:             : undefined;
311:           continue;
312:         }
313:         residual += `${frame}\n`;
314:       }
315:       if (!initialized) return;
316:       const remainder = residual + buffer;
317:       buffer = '';
318:       residual = '';
319:       cleanup();
320:       resolve({ residual: remainder, protocolVersion: negotiatedVersion, handshakeObserved: true, initializeResponse });
321:     };
322:     const onError = (error) => {
323:       cleanup();
324:       reject(error);
325:     };
326:     const onClose = () => {
327:       cleanup();
328:       reject(new Error('socket closed during internal initialize'));
329:     };
330: 
331:     socket.on('data', onData);
332:     socket.once('error', onError);
333:     socket.once('close', onClose);
334:     writeFrame(socket, initializeFrame);
335:   });
336: }

338: function createSessionProxy(options) {

572:   function handleClientFrame(frame) {

693:   async function reattach() {

703:         } catch (error) {

17: let signalHandlersInstalled = false;
18: const DEFAULT_WRITER_LOCK_MAX_WAIT_MS = 1_000;
19: const DEFAULT_WRITER_LOCK_RETRY_INTERVAL_MS = 25;
20: 
21: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
22: // 3. HELPERS
23: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
24: 
25: function findRepoRoot(startDir) {
26:   let current = path.resolve(startDir);
27:   let opencodeMatch = null;
28:   while (current !== path.dirname(current)) {
29:     // `.git` is the unambiguous repo-root marker; prefer it so nested `.opencode`
30:     // sandbox/residue dirs under skills/** cannot short-circuit detection.
31:     if (fs.existsSync(path.join(current, '.git'))) {
32:       return current;
33:     }
34:     if (opencodeMatch === null && fs.existsSync(path.join(current, '.opencode'))) {
35:       opencodeMatch = current;
36:     }
37:     current = path.dirname(current);
38:   }
39:   return opencodeMatch || path.resolve(startDir, '..', '..', '..', '..', '..');
40: }
41: 
42: const REPO_ROOT = findRepoRoot(__dirname);
43: 
44: function hasTraversalSegment(value) {
45:   return value.split(/[\\/]+/).some((segment) => segment === '..');
46: }
47: 
48: function isInsideRepo(resolvedPath) {
49:   const relative = path.relative(REPO_ROOT, resolvedPath);
50:   return relative === '' || (!relative.startsWith('..') && !path.isAbsolute(relative));
51: }
52: 
53: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
54: // 4. CORE LOGIC
55: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
56: 
57: /**
58:  * Validate a namespace-scoped value (specFolder, sessionId) for safety.
59:  * Rejects null bytes, `..` path traversal segments, and absolute paths
60:  * that escape the repository root.
61:  *
62:  * @param {string} value - The value to validate.
63:  * @param {string} key - Label for error messages (e.g. 'specFolder').
64:  * @param {Function} makeInputError - Factory that creates an InputValidation
65:  *   error from a message string.
66:  * @returns {string} The validated value, unchanged when valid.
67:  * @throws {Error} With code INPUT_VALIDATION on failure.
68:  */
69: function validateNamespaceValue(value, key, makeInputError) {
70:   if (value.includes('\0')) {
71:     throw makeInputError(`${key} must not contain null bytes`);
72:   }
73:   if (hasTraversalSegment(value)) {
74:     throw makeInputError(`${key} must not contain '..' path segments`);
75:   }
76:   if (path.isAbsolute(value) && !isInsideRepo(path.resolve(value))) {
77:     throw makeInputError(`${key} absolute path must stay inside the repository root`);
78:   }
79:   return value;
80: }
81: 
82: /**
83:  * Install one-shot signal handlers (SIGINT, SIGTERM) that run a cleanup
84:  * callback before exiting. Idempotent — subsequent calls are no-ops.
85:  *
86:  * @param {Function} cleanup - Cleanup callback invoked before process exit.
87:  */
88: function installSignalHandlers(cleanup) {
89:   if (signalHandlersInstalled) return;
90:   signalHandlersInstalled = true;
91: 
92:   for (const signal of ['SIGINT', 'SIGTERM']) {
93:     process.once(signal, () => {
94:       try {
95:         cleanup();
96:       } finally {
97:         process.exit(signal === 'SIGINT' ? 130 : 143);
98:       }
99:     });
100:   }
101: }
102: 
103: /**
104:  * Map an error to a structured exit code for CLI scripts.
105:  *
106:  * @param {Error|Object} err - An error object, optionally carrying a `code`
107:  *   property.
108:  * @returns {number} Exit code: 1=script error, 2=DB/permissions error,
109:  *   3=input validation error.
110:  */
111: function classifyExitCode(err) {
112:   const code = err && typeof err === 'object' && 'code' in err ? err.code : undefined;
113:   if (code === 'INPUT_VALIDATION') return 3;
114:   if (
115:     code === 'DB_ERROR'
116:     || code === 'SQLITE_ERROR'
117:     || code === 'SQLITE_BUSY'
118:     || code === 'SQLITE_CANTOPEN'
119:     || code === 'EACCES'
120:     || code === 'EPERM'
121:     || code === 'ENOTDIR'
122:   ) {
123:     return 2;
124:   }
125:   return 1;
126: }
127: 
128: /**
129:  * Throw a synthetic fault when the DEEP_LOOP_TEST_FAULT environment
130:  * variable is set. Used by contract tests to verify that scripts
131:  * handle injected DB and script-level errors correctly.
132:  *
133:  * @throws {Error} With code DB_ERROR or generic script error.
134:  */
135: function maybeThrowTestFault() {
136:   if (process.env.DEEP_LOOP_TEST_FAULT === 'db') {
137:     const err = new Error('Injected DB fault for deep-loop script contract tests');
138:     err.code = 'DB_ERROR';
139:     throw err;
140:   }
141:   if (process.env.DEEP_LOOP_TEST_FAULT === 'script') {
142:     throw new Error('Injected script fault for deep-loop script contract tests');
143:   }
144: }
145: 
146: /**
147:  * Synchronous sleep using Atomics.wait on a shared buffer.
148:  *
149:  * @param {number} ms - Milliseconds to sleep. Non-positive values are
150:  *   ignored.
151:  */
152: function sleepSync(ms) {
153:   if (!Number.isFinite(ms) || ms <= 0) return;
154:   const buffer = new SharedArrayBuffer(4);
155:   const view = new Int32Array(buffer);
156:   Atomics.wait(view, 0, 0, ms);
157: }
158: 
159: function readNonNegativeEnvNumber(name, fallback) {
160:   const raw = process.env[name];
161:   if (raw === undefined || raw === '') return fallback;
162:   const value = Number(raw);
163:   return Number.isFinite(value) && value >= 0 ? value : fallback;
164: }
165: 
166: /**
167:  * Acquire an exclusive writer lock via `wx` file open on the given path.
168:  * Returns a release function that closes the file descriptor and removes
169:  * the lock file. Retries briefly on contention so parallel test files and
170:  * near-simultaneous runtime callers do not fail on transient writer overlap.
171:  *
172:  * @param {string} lockPath - Path to the lock file to create.
173:  * @returns {Function} Release function (call to unlock and clean up).
174:  * @throws {Error} With code DB_ERROR if the lock remains held after retry.
175:  */
176: function acquireWriterLock(lockPath) {
177:   fs.mkdirSync(path.dirname(lockPath), { recursive: true });
178:   const maxWaitMs = readNonNegativeEnvNumber('DEEP_LOOP_WRITER_LOCK_MAX_WAIT_MS', DEFAULT_WRITER_LOCK_MAX_WAIT_MS);
179:   const retryIntervalMs = readNonNegativeEnvNumber('DEEP_LOOP_WRITER_LOCK_RETRY_INTERVAL_MS', DEFAULT_WRITER_LOCK_RETRY_INTERVAL_MS);
180:   const deadline = Date.now() + maxWaitMs;
181:   let fd;
182: 
183:   while (true) {
184:     try {
185:       fd = fs.openSync(lockPath, 'wx');
186:       break;
187:     } catch (err) {
188:       const code = err && typeof err === 'object' && 'code' in err ? err.code : undefined;
189:       if (code !== 'EEXIST') throw err;
190: 
191:       const remainingMs = deadline - Date.now();
192:       if (maxWaitMs <= 0 || remainingMs <= 0) {
193:         const lockErr = new Error('deep-loop graph writer lock is held by another process');
194:         lockErr.code = 'DB_ERROR';
195:         throw lockErr;
196:       }
197:       sleepSync(Math.min(retryIntervalMs, remainingMs));
198:     }
199:   }
200: 
201:   return () => {
202:     if (typeof fd === 'number') {
203:       try {
204:         fs.closeSync(fd);
205:       } catch {
206:         // Best-effort cleanup during process teardown.
207:       }
208:       fd = undefined;
209:     }
210:     fs.rmSync(lockPath, { force: true });
211:   };
212: }
213: 
214: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
215: // 5. EXPORTS
216: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
217: 
218: module.exports = {
219:   acquireWriterLock,
220:   classifyExitCode,
221:   installSignalHandlers,
222:   maybeThrowTestFault,
223:   sleepSync,
224:   validateNamespaceValue,
225: };

1: // ╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
2: // ║ Deep-Loop Runtime — Fan-Out Worker Pool                                   ║
3: // ╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
4: // ║ Concurrency-capped fan-out primitive for the opt-in multi-executor        ║
5: // ║ ("fan-out") layer above the single-executor deep-loop. Generalizes the    ║
6: // ║ council parallel dispatcher (lib/council/multi-seat-dispatch.cjs) by      ║
7: // ║ adding a concurrency cap so N executor lineages run with at most K in      ║
8: // ║ flight, plus a status-ledger writer following the proven                  ║
9: // ║ orchestration-status.log ledger pattern.                                   ║
10: // ║                                                                           ║
11: // ║ Design: pure pool primitive (worker is INJECTED) + ledger helpers, fully  ║
12: // ║ unit-tested. The real spawn worker and the CLI entry that wires it        ║
13: // ║ (arg parse, JSON-out, exit codes) live in fanout-run.cjs.                ║
14: // ╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
15: 
16: 'use strict';
17: 
18: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
19: // 1. IMPORTS
20: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
21: 
22: const fs = require('node:fs');
23: const path = require('node:path');
24: 
25: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
26: // 2. HELPERS
27: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
28: 
29: function isRecord(value) {
30:   return value !== null && typeof value === 'object' && !Array.isArray(value);
31: }
32: 
33: function normalizeTimestamp(value) {
34:   if (value instanceof Date) {
35:     return value.toISOString();
36:   }
37:   if (typeof value === 'string' && value.trim() !== '') {
38:     return value;
39:   }
40:   return new Date().toISOString();
41: }
42: 
43: function normalizeConcurrency(value) {
44:   const n = Number(value);
45:   if (!Number.isFinite(n) || n < 1) {
46:     return 1;
47:   }
48:   return Math.floor(n);
49: }
50: 
51: function labelFor(item, index) {
52:   if (isRecord(item) && typeof item.label === 'string' && item.label.trim() !== '') {
53:     return item.label;
54:   }
55:   return `item-${index}`;
56: }
57: 
58: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
59: // 3. CORE LOGIC
60: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
61: 
62: /**
63:  * Run a single pool item through the worker and capture its result or error.
64:  *
65:  * Never throws: a worker rejection is captured as a `rejected` result so one
66:  * failing lineage cannot sink the whole pool (mirrors council `settleSeat`).
67:  *
68:  * @param {Object} params - Settlement parameters.
69:  * @param {*} params.item - The work item (e.g. a lineage descriptor).
70:  * @param {number} params.index - Zero-based item index (preserves order).
71:  * @param {Function} params.worker - Async worker: (item, { index }) => output.
72:  * @param {Function} params.now - Callable returning a Date or ISO timestamp.
73:  * @param {Function} [params.onEvent] - Optional callback for ledger events.
74:  * @returns {Promise<Object>} Result with label, status, timing, output|error.
75:  */
76: async function settleItem({ item, index, worker, now, onEvent }) {
77:   const label = labelFor(item, index);
78:   const startedAtIso = normalizeTimestamp(now());
79:   const startedAtMs = Date.now();
80: 
81:   if (typeof onEvent === 'function') {
82:     onEvent({ event: 'started', label, index, at: startedAtIso });
83:   }
84: 
85:   try {
86:     const output = await worker(item, { index });
87:     const completedAtIso = normalizeTimestamp(now());
88:     const durationMs = Math.max(0, Date.now() - startedAtMs);
89:     if (typeof onEvent === 'function') {
90:       onEvent({ event: 'completed', label, index, at: completedAtIso, duration_ms: durationMs });
91:     }
92:     return {
93:       label,
94:       status: 'fulfilled',
95:       started_at_iso: startedAtIso,
96:       completed_at_iso: completedAtIso,
97:       duration_ms: durationMs,
98:       output,
99:     };
100:   } catch (error) {
101:     const completedAtIso = normalizeTimestamp(now());
102:     const durationMs = Math.max(0, Date.now() - startedAtMs);
103:     const normalizedError = {
104:       name: error && error.name ? String(error.name) : 'Error',
105:       message: error && error.message ? String(error.message) : String(error),
106:     };
107:     if (typeof onEvent === 'function') {
108:       onEvent({ event: 'failed', label, index, at: completedAtIso, duration_ms: durationMs, error: normalizedError });
109:     }
110:     return {
111:       label,
112:       status: 'rejected',
113:       started_at_iso: startedAtIso,
114:       completed_at_iso: completedAtIso,
115:       duration_ms: durationMs,
116:       error: normalizedError,
117:     };
118:   }
119: }
120: 
121: /**
122:  * Run items through a worker with at most `concurrency` in flight.
123:  *
124:  * This is the fan-out generalization of council `dispatchCouncilSeats`: same
125:  * never-throws per-item settlement and ordered results, plus a concurrency cap
126:  * so a large lineage set runs K-at-a-time (the proven `xargs -P K` shape).
127:  *
128:  * @param {Object} options - Pool configuration.
129:  * @param {Array} options.items - Work items (each ideally has a `label`).
130:  * @param {number} options.concurrency - Max items in flight (clamped to >= 1).
131:  * @param {Function} options.worker - Async worker: (item, { index }) => output.
132:  * @param {Function} [options.now] - Callable returning a Date/ISO timestamp.
133:  * @param {Function} [options.onEvent] - Optional ledger-event callback.
134:  * @returns {Promise<Object>} { results: [...ordered], summary: { total,
135:  *   succeeded, failed, all_failed } }.
136:  * @throws {TypeError} If items is not an array or worker is not a function.
137:  */
138: function runCappedPool(options) {
139:   if (!isRecord(options)) {
140:     throw new TypeError('runCappedPool options must be an object');
141:   }
142:   const { items, worker } = options;
143:   if (!Array.isArray(items)) {
144:     throw new TypeError('items must be an array');
145:   }
146:   if (typeof worker !== 'function') {
147:     throw new TypeError('worker must be a function');
148:   }
149: 
150:   const concurrency = normalizeConcurrency(options.concurrency);
151:   const now = typeof options.now === 'function' ? options.now : () => new Date();
152:   const onEvent = typeof options.onEvent === 'function' ? options.onEvent : undefined;
153:   const results = new Array(items.length);
154: 
155:   return new Promise((resolve) => {
156:     if (items.length === 0) {
157:       resolve(buildPoolSummary(results));
158:       return;
159:     }
160: 
161:     let nextIndex = 0;
162:     let active = 0;
163:     let resolved = false;
164: 
165:     const pump = () => {
166:       if (resolved) {
167:         return;
168:       }
169:       if (nextIndex >= items.length && active === 0) {
170:         resolved = true;
171:         resolve(buildPoolSummary(results));
172:         return;
173:       }
174:       while (active < concurrency && nextIndex < items.length) {
175:         const index = nextIndex;
176:         nextIndex += 1;
177:         active += 1;
178:         settleItem({ item: items[index], index, worker, now, onEvent })
179:           .then((result) => {
180:             results[index] = result;
181:           })
182:           .catch((error) => {
183:             // settleItem never throws, but stay defensive so the pump never wedges.
184:             results[index] = {
185:               label: labelFor(items[index], index),
186:               status: 'rejected',
187:               error: { name: 'PoolError', message: String(error) },
188:             };
189:           })
190:           .finally(() => {
191:             active -= 1;
192:             pump();
193:           });
194:       }
195:     };
196: 
197:     pump();
198:   });
199: }
200: 
201: /**
202:  * Build the ordered-results + summary envelope for a completed pool run.
203:  *
204:  * @param {Array<Object>} results - Per-item settlement results (ordered).
205:  * @returns {Object} { results, summary: { total, succeeded, failed, all_failed } }.
206:  */
207: function buildPoolSummary(results) {
208:   const total = results.length;
209:   const succeeded = results.filter((result) => result && result.status === 'fulfilled').length;
210:   const failed = total - succeeded;
211:   return {
212:     results,
213:     summary: {
214:       total,
215:       succeeded,
216:       failed,
217:       all_failed: total > 0 && failed === total,
218:     },
219:   };
220: }
221: 
222: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
223: // 4. STATUS LEDGER
224: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
225: 
226: /**
227:  * Append one JSONL event to the orchestration status ledger.
228:  *
229:  * Follows the `orchestration-status.log` ledger pattern: one line per lineage
230:  * lifecycle event (started / completed / failed / salvaged / converged).
231:  *
232:  * @param {string} ledgerPath - Path to the JSONL ledger file.
233:  * @param {Object} entry - A serializable event object.
234:  */
235: function appendStatusLedger(ledgerPath, entry) {
236:   fs.mkdirSync(path.dirname(ledgerPath), { recursive: true });
237:   fs.appendFileSync(ledgerPath, `${JSON.stringify(entry)}\n`);
238: }
239: 
240: /**
241:  * Write the orchestration summary JSON for a completed fan-out run.
242:  *
243:  * @param {string} summaryPath - Path to the summary JSON file.
244:  * @param {Object} summary - Summary payload (e.g. the pool summary plus
245:  *   salvage counts and per-lineage rollups).
246:  */
247: function writeOrchestrationSummary(summaryPath, summary) {
248:   fs.mkdirSync(path.dirname(summaryPath), { recursive: true });
249:   fs.writeFileSync(summaryPath, `${JSON.stringify(summary, null, 2)}\n`);
250: }
251: 
252: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
253: // 5. EXPORTS
254: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
255: 
256: module.exports = {
257:   runCappedPool,
258:   settleItem,
259:   buildPoolSummary,
260:   appendStatusLedger,
261:   writeOrchestrationSummary,
262: };

display.text:
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# DOCTOR: MEMORY CONTINUITY-INDEX WORKFLOW (INTERACTIVE DIAGNOSTIC MODE)
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
role: Expert Operator running memory continuity-index diagnostics in interactive mode
purpose: Read-only health check + drift detection with user review gates. No mutations. Report goes to packet-local scratch.
action: Discovery → gate → analysis → gate → drift classification → recommendation → state log

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# OPERATING MODE
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
operating_mode:
  workflow: sequential
  compliance: MANDATORY
  execution: interactive
  approvals: per_phase
  tracking: phase_by_phase
  validation: continuous_self_validation
  intent: DIAGNOSE  # never APPLY in this YAML


memory_doctor_invariant: |
  Memory doctor is read-only by contract. It treats the active provider-keyed
  profile DBs (ollama, hf-local, voyage, and openai profiles matching
  context-index__*.sqlite) as the memory-owned stores it diagnoses but never
  mutates. Rebuilds are owned by the /doctor:update orchestrator, which relies on
  the per-file memory_index_scan transaction model so SIGINT uses the ~5 sec settle
  window before restoring snapshots for cross-file consistency.

upstream_assets:
  contract: "local command design contract"
  files:
    spec: "spec.md"  # REQ-005..REQ-007 cover flock, snapshots, dashboard
    decision_record: "decision-record.md"  # ADR-001 documents memory_index_scan tx model
    tx_model: "decision-record.md#adr-001"  # per-file transactions + ~5 sec SIGINT settle
    checklist: "checklist.md#doctor-memory"  # CHK-104 gold-battery acceptance bar
  pass_policy:
    minimum_result_count_per_query: 1  # existing memory gold-battery floor
    post_result_count_floor_ratio: 0.80  # preserve current workflow threshold
    recall_at_20_floor_ratio: 0.80  # preserve current Recall@20 proxy
memory_doctor_philosophy:
  principle: "Detect drift before it breaks memory_search; this command never mutates — rebuilds run through /doctor:update"
  approach: "Read memory_health + memory_stats → classify drift signals → emit recommendations"
  mandate: "This command is read-only by contract."

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# USER INPUTS (passed from Markdown setup phase)
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
user_inputs:
  execution_mode: "[EXECUTION_MODE] - INTERACTIVE (this YAML)"
  intent: "[INTENT] - DIAGNOSE (this YAML)"
  incremental: "[INCREMENTAL] - true|false (informational; no rebuild in this command)"

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# MUTATION BOUNDARIES (READ-ONLY)
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

field_handling:
  defaults:
    incremental_empty: "false"
  incremental_policy:
    true: "diagnostic input only; recommend incremental apply when medium drift is detected"
    false: "diagnostic input only; recommend full apply when high-severity drift is detected"
mutation_boundaries:
  allowed_targets:
    - "mcp_server/database/context-index__*.sqlite"  # provider-keyed active profile DBs
    - "mcp_server/database/context-index*.sqlite.pre-doctor-memory.*.bak"  # memory snapshot files only
  forbidden_targets:
    - "<active-spec-folder>/**/*.md"  # never write into spec packet docs from /doctor memory
    - "<active-spec-folder>/description.json"  # spec metadata belongs to memory-save tooling
    - "<active-spec-folder>/graph-metadata.json"  # graph metadata is not a memory doctor target
    - ".opencode/skills/**/SKILL.md"  # never edit skill bodies
    - ".opencode/skills/**/graph-metadata.json"
    - ".opencode/agents/**/*.md"
    - ".opencode/commands/doctor/*.md"
    - ".opencode/commands/doctor/assets/doctor_*.yaml"
    - "mcp_server/database/*code-graph*"
    - "mcp_server/database/*deep-loop*"
    - "mcp_server/database/*eval*"
    - "mcp_server/database/*skill*"
    - "mcp_server/database/*causal*"

<path>/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-salvage.cjs</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: // ╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
2: // ║ Deep-Loop Runtime — Fan-Out Lineage Salvage                              ║
3: // ╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
4: // ║ Recovers missing iteration files from captured subprocess stdout when    ║
5: // ║ a CLI executor fails to write outputs (sandbox write restrictions, etc). ║
6: // ╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
7: 
8: 'use strict';
9: 
10: const fs = require('node:fs');
11: const path = require('node:path');
12: 
13: const STATE_LOG_BY_LOOP_TYPE = {
14:   research: 'deep-research-state.jsonl',
15:   review: 'deep-review-state.jsonl',
16: };
17: 
18: /**
19:  * Parse opencode --format json text parts from subprocess stdout.
20:  * Opencode emits JSONL lines of {type:"text",part:{text:"..."}}.
21:  * Concatenates all text parts; falls back to raw stdout for other executors.
22:  *
23:  * @param {string|null} stdout - Combined subprocess stdout.
24:  * @returns {string|null} Recovered text, or null if nothing substantive.
25:  */
26: function extractTextFromOpencodeJson(stdout) {
27:   if (!stdout || typeof stdout !== 'string') return null;
28: 
29:   const textParts = [];
30:   for (const line of stdout.split('\n')) {
31:     const trimmed = line.trim();
32:     if (!trimmed.startsWith('{')) continue;
33:     try {
34:       const parsed = JSON.parse(trimmed);
35:       if (
36:         parsed &&
37:         parsed.type === 'text' &&
38:         parsed.part &&
39:         typeof parsed.part.text === 'string'
40:       ) {
41:         textParts.push(parsed.part.text);
42:       }
43:     } catch {
44:       // non-JSON line — skip
45:     }
46:   }
47: 
48:   if (textParts.length > 0) return textParts.join('').slice(0, 50_000);
49: 
50:   const raw = stdout.trim();
51:   return raw.length > 50 ? raw.slice(0, 50_000) : null;
52: }
53: 
54: /**
55:  * After a lineage subprocess exits, recover any missing or empty iteration
56:  * files from the saved stdout (write-failure salvage path).
57:  *
58:  * For each iteration recorded in the state log that lacks its .md file:
59:  *   - If recoverable text is available: write it and append a
60:  *     salvaged_from_stdout event to the state log.
61:  *   - Otherwise: write a failed-marker placeholder so downstream steps
62:  *     see a file and can apply their own stuck-recovery logic.
63:  *
64:  * @param {string} lineageDir - Absolute path to the lineage artifact dir.
65:  * @param {'research'|'review'} loopType - Loop type for state log naming.
66:  * @param {string} savedStdout - Captured subprocess stdout.
67:  * @returns {{ salvaged: number, failed: number }}
68:  */
69: function runSalvageSweep(lineageDir, loopType, savedStdout) {
70:   const stateLogName = STATE_LOG_BY_LOOP_TYPE[loopType];
71:   if (!stateLogName) return { salvaged: 0, failed: 0 };
72: 
73:   const stateLogPath = path.join(lineageDir, stateLogName);
74:   const iterDir = path.join(lineageDir, 'iterations');
75: 
76:   if (!fs.existsSync(stateLogPath)) return { salvaged: 0, failed: 0 };
77: 
78:   // Discover which iterations completed by scanning the state log.
79:   const iterationNumbers = new Set();
80:   let stateContent;

1: ---
2: title: Skill Advisor Hook Reference
3: description: Operator contract for the native-first Skill Advisor hooks across Claude Code, Copilot CLI, Gemini CLI, Codex CLI, and the OpenCode plugin bridge.
4: ---
5: 
6: # Skill Advisor Hook Reference
7: 
8: This reference describes the current prompt-time Skill Advisor integrations. The primary routing implementation is the native TypeScript package at `.opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/`; Python remains as a compatibility fallback through `.opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/scripts/skill_advisor.py`.
9: 
10: ---
11: 
12: ## 1. OVERVIEW
13: 
14: ### Purpose
15: 
16: Define the prompt-time Skill Advisor hook contract across Claude Code, Copilot CLI, Gemini CLI, Codex CLI, and the OpenCode plugin bridge.
17: 
18: ### When to Use
19: 
20: Load this reference when wiring runtime hooks, smoke-testing advisor brief output, checking threshold behavior, or diagnosing freshness and fail-open states.
21: 
22: ### Core Principle
23: 
24: Hooks only surface prompt-safe routing context. They do not replace explicit skill loading, persist raw prompt text, or block the user prompt on advisor failures.
25: 
26: ### Default Flow
27: 
28: The hook layer injects a compact advisor brief before the model responds. It does not replace skill loading; it only surfaces the likely skill route and the freshness state.
29: 
30: Default flow:
31: 
32: 1. Runtime receives a prompt.
33: 2. Hook adapter parses the prompt payload.
34: 3. Adapter calls `buildSkillAdvisorBrief(prompt, { runtime, workspaceRoot })`.
35: 4. Native advisor is used when live or stale.
36: 5. Compatibility fallback is used when native routing is unavailable.
37: 6. Renderer returns a short `Advisor: ...` brief or an empty fail-open result.
38: 7. Diagnostics are written without raw prompt text.
39: 
40: When the prompt resumes a phase-parent target and `graph-metadata.json` has a fresh `derived.last_active_child_id` pointer (non-null and `derived.last_active_at` within 24 hours), the brief surfaces that active child as the next-action target instead of the parent's child listing. Missing, null, stale, or invalid pointers fall back to the parent control file/listing behavior.
41: 
42: Native tool baseline:
43: 
44: | Tool | Purpose |
45: | --- | --- |
46: | `advisor_recommend` | Prompt-safe skill recommendations with explicit `workspaceRoot`, lane attribution, lifecycle metadata, and effective thresholds. |
47: | `advisor_status` | Diagnostic-only freshness, generation, trust state, `skillCount`, `lastScanAt`, and lane weights. It does not rebuild. |
48: | `advisor_rebuild` | Explicit repair command for stale, absent, or unavailable advisor state; pass `force:true` to rebuild even when status is live. |
49: | `advisor_validate` | Corpus, holdout, parity, safety, latency, threshold semantics, and prompt-safe telemetry rollups. |
50: 
51: ---
52: 
53: ## 2. RUNTIME MATRIX
54: 
55: | Runtime | Source Hook | Output Shape | Notes |
56: | --- | --- | --- | --- |
57: | Claude Code | `mcp_server/hooks/claude/user-prompt-submit.ts` | `hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext` | Reads `prompt` and `cwd`. |
58: | Gemini CLI | `mcp_server/hooks/gemini/user-prompt-submit.ts` | `hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext` | Reads `prompt`, `userPrompt`, or `request.prompt`. |
59: | Codex CLI | `mcp_server/hooks/codex/user-prompt-submit.ts` | `hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext` | Stdin JSON is canonical and wins over argv JSON. |
60: | Codex fallback | `mcp_server/hooks/codex/prompt-wrapper.ts` | `promptWrapper` and `wrappedPrompt` | Runs only when Codex hook policy reports hooks unavailable. |
61: | OpenCode | `.opencode/plugins/mk-skill-advisor.js` + `.opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/plugin_bridges/mk-skill-advisor-bridge.mjs` | `experimental.chat.system.transform` mutates `output.system` | Bridge imports native `compat/index.js`, applies the same effective threshold on native and fallback paths, then falls back to the Python-backed brief path only when native is unavailable. |
62: 
63: Build all runtime adapters (both steps required):
64: 
65: ```bash
66: # 1. Builds the system-spec-kit hook shims.
67: npm --prefix .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server run build
68: # 2. Builds the real adapter targets that those shims spawn.
69: npm --prefix .opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server run build
70: ```
71: 
72: ---
73: 
74: ## 3. SHARED BEHAVIOR
75: 
76: All hook adapters:
77: 
78: - fail open with `{}` or no context when parsing, native status, scoring, or rendering fails
79: - honor `SPECKIT_SKILL_ADVISOR_HOOK_DISABLED=1`
80: - avoid persisting raw prompts in diagnostics, cache metadata, status, or attribution
81: - use the same freshness vocabulary: `live`, `stale`, `absent`, `unavailable`
82: - use the same status vocabulary: `ok`, `skipped`, `degraded`, `fail_open`
83: - render a compact `Advisor: ...` brief only when a route passes threshold
84: - use `0.8 / 0.35` as the default prompt-time confidence/uncertainty routing thresholds unless a caller explicitly overrides the confidence threshold
85: - persist prompt-safe hook diagnostics across hook processes and exclude raw prompt text from the durable sink
86: 
87: Native `advisor_recommend` returns prompt-safe lane contribution metadata when `includeAttribution` is true. It does not return prompt-derived evidence snippets.
88: 
89: ---
90: 
91: ## 4. SETUP AND SMOKE TESTS
92: 
93: ### Claude Code
94: 
95: ```bash
96: printf '%s' '{"prompt":"help me commit my changes","cwd":"'"$PWD"'","hook_event_name":"UserPromptSubmit"}' | \
97:   node .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/dist/hooks/claude/user-prompt-submit.js
98: ```
99: 
100: Expected: `{}` or `hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext` beginning with `Advisor:`.
101: 
102: ### Gemini CLI
103: 
104: ```bash
105: printf '%s' '{"request":{"prompt":"create a flowchart for the auth process","cwd":"'"$PWD"'"}}' | \
106:   node .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/dist/hooks/gemini/user-prompt-submit.js
107: ```
108: 
109: Expected: `{}` or `hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext`.

114: printf '%s' '{"prompt":"update documentation with DQI checks","cwd":"'"$PWD"'"}' | \

116: ```

display.text:
---
title: Skill Advisor Hook Reference
description: Operator contract for the native-first Skill Advisor hooks across Claude Code, Copilot CLI, Gemini CLI, Codex CLI, and the OpenCode plugin bridge.
---

# Skill Advisor Hook Reference

This reference describes the current prompt-time Skill Advisor integrations. The primary routing implementation is the native TypeScript package at `.opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/`; Python remains as a compatibility fallback through `.opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/scripts/skill_advisor.py`.

---

## 1. OVERVIEW

### Purpose

Define the prompt-time Skill Advisor hook contract across Claude Code, Copilot CLI, Gemini CLI, Codex CLI, and the OpenCode plugin bridge.

### When to Use

Load this reference when wiring runtime hooks, smoke-testing advisor brief output, checking threshold behavior, or diagnosing freshness and fail-open states.

### Core Principle

Hooks only surface prompt-safe routing context. They do not replace explicit skill loading, persist raw prompt text, or block the user prompt on advisor failures.

### Default Flow

The hook layer injects a compact advisor brief before the model responds. It does not replace skill loading; it only surfaces the likely skill route and the freshness state.

Default flow:

1. Runtime receives a prompt.
2. Hook adapter parses the prompt payload.
3. Adapter calls `buildSkillAdvisorBrief(prompt, { runtime, workspaceRoot })`.
4. Native advisor is used when live or stale.
5. Compatibility fallback is used when native routing is unavailable.
6. Renderer returns a short `Advisor: ...` brief or an empty fail-open result.
7. Diagnostics are written without raw prompt text.

When the prompt resumes a phase-parent target and `graph-metadata.json` has a fresh `derived.last_active_child_id` pointer (non-null and `derived.last_active_at` within 24 hours), the brief surfaces that active child as the next-action target instead of the parent's child listing. Missing, null, stale, or invalid pointers fall back to the parent control file/listing behavior.

Native tool baseline:

| Tool | Purpose |
| --- | --- |
| `advisor_recommend` | Prompt-safe skill recommendations with explicit `workspaceRoot`, lane attribution, lifecycle metadata, and effective thresholds. |
| `advisor_status` | Diagnostic-only freshness, generation, trust state, `skillCount`, `lastScanAt`, and lane weights. It does not rebuild. |
| `advisor_rebuild` | Explicit repair command for stale, absent, or unavailable advisor state; pass `force:true` to rebuild even when status is live. |
| `advisor_validate` | Corpus, holdout, parity, safety, latency, threshold semantics, and prompt-safe telemetry rollups. |

---

## 2. RUNTIME MATRIX

| Runtime | Source Hook | Output Shape | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Claude Code | `mcp_server/hooks/claude/user-prompt-submit.ts` | `hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext` | Reads `prompt` and `cwd`. |
| Gemini CLI | `mcp_server/hooks/gemini/user-prompt-submit.ts` | `hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext` | Reads `prompt`, `userPrompt`, or `request.prompt`. |
| Codex CLI | `mcp_server/hooks/codex/user-prompt-submit.ts` | `hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext` | Stdin JSON is canonical and wins over argv JSON. |
| Codex fallback | `mcp_server/hooks/codex/prompt-wrapper.ts` | `promptWrapper` and `wrappedPrompt` | Runs only when Codex hook policy reports hooks unavailable. |
| OpenCode | `.opencode/plugins/mk-skill-advisor.js` + `.opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/plugin_bridges/mk-skill-advisor-bridge.mjs` | `experimental.chat.system.transform` mutates `output.system` | Bridge imports native `compat/index.js`, applies the same effective threshold on native and fallback paths, then falls back to the Python-backed brief path only when native is unavailable. |

Build all runtime adapters (both steps required):

```bash
# 1. Builds the system-spec-kit hook shims.
npm --prefix .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server run build
# 2. Builds the real adapter targets that those shims spawn.
npm --prefix .opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server run build
```

---

## 3. SHARED BEHAVIOR

All hook adapters:

- fail open with `{}` or no context when parsing, native status, scoring, or rendering fails
- honor `SPECKIT_SKILL_ADVISOR_HOOK_DISABLED=1`
- avoid persisting raw prompts in diagnostics, cache metadata, status, or attribution
- use the same freshness vocabulary: `live`, `stale`, `absent`, `unavailable`
- use the same status vocabulary: `ok`, `skipped`, `degraded`, `fail_open`
- render a compact `Advisor: ...` brief only when a route passes threshold
- use `0.8 / 0.35` as the default prompt-time confidence/uncertainty routing thresholds unless a caller explicitly overrides the confidence threshold
- persist prompt-safe hook diagnostics across hook processes and exclude raw prompt text from the durable sink

Native `advisor_recommend` returns prompt-safe lane contribution metadata when `includeAttribution` is true. It does not return prompt-derived evidence snippets.

---

## 4. SETUP AND SMOKE TESTS

### Claude Code

```bash
printf '%s' '{"prompt":"help me commit my changes","cwd":"'"$PWD"'","hook_event_name":"UserPromptSubmit"}' | \
  node .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/dist/hooks/claude/user-prompt-submit.js
```

Expected: `{}` or `hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext` beginning with `Advisor:`.

### Gemini CLI

```bash
printf '%s' '{"request":{"prompt":"create a flowchart for the auth process","cwd":"'"$PWD"'"}}' | \
  node .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/dist/hooks/gemini/user-prompt-submit.js
```

Expected: `{}` or `hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext`.

### Codex CLI

```bash
printf '%s' '{"prompt":"update documentation with DQI checks","cwd":"'"$PWD"'"}' | \
  node .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/dist/hooks/codex/user-prompt-submit.js
```

Prompt wrapper fallback:

```bash

24: deep_loop_doctor_invariant: |
25:   Deep-loop doctor is read-only by contract. It reads graph nodes and edges and
26:   the research/review iteration folders to assess coverage, without modifying the
27:   source markdown files or the coverage graph. It never calls

# DOCTOR: DEEP LOOP COVERAGE GRAPH WORKFLOW (INTERACTIVE DIAGNOSTIC MODE)
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
role: Expert Operator running deep-loop coverage graph diagnostics in interactive mode
purpose: Read-only deep-loop graph status and lazy-init analysis with user review gates. No mutations.
action: Discovery -> gate -> analysis -> gate -> recommendation -> report

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# OPERATING MODE
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
operating_mode:
  workflow: sequential
  compliance: MANDATORY
  execution: interactive
  approvals: per_phase
  tracking: phase_by_phase

display.text:
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# DOCTOR: DEEP LOOP COVERAGE GRAPH WORKFLOW (INTERACTIVE DIAGNOSTIC MODE)
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
role: Expert Operator running deep-loop coverage graph diagnostics in interactive mode
purpose: Read-only deep-loop graph status and lazy-init analysis with user review gates. No mutations.
action: Discovery -> gate -> analysis -> gate -> recommendation -> report

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# OPERATING MODE
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
operating_mode:
  workflow: sequential
  compliance: MANDATORY
  execution: interactive
  approvals: per_phase
  tracking: phase_by_phase
  validation: continuous_self_validation
  intent: DIAGNOSE


# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# DEEP LOOP DOCTOR INVARIANT
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
deep_loop_doctor_invariant: |
  Deep-loop doctor is read-only by contract. It reads graph nodes and edges and
  the research/review iteration folders to assess coverage, without modifying the
  source markdown files or the coverage graph. It never calls
  deep_loop_graph_upsert; rebuilds are owned by /doctor:update. Lazy-init is
  surfaced only as a recommendation when the graph is empty and iteration folders
  provide a readable source inventory.

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# UPSTREAM ASSETS
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
upstream_assets:
  contract: "local command design contract"
  files:
    spec: "spec.md"  # REQ-013 covers lazy-init/default-mode behavior
    decision_record: "decision-record.md"  # ADR-004/ADR-007 rollback and cancel safety
    checklist: "checklist.md#doctor-deep-loop"  # CHK-304 convergence acceptance
  pass_policy:
    convergence_signal_required: true  # CHK-304 gold-battery signal
    minimum_iterations: 3  # existing gold_battery.minimum_iterations
    lazy_init_source_required: true  # REQ-013 source detection before upsert

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# DEEP LOOP DOCTOR PHILOSOPHY
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
deep_loop_doctor_philosophy:
  principle: "Coverage graph reads should prove whether research/review iteration evidence is represented without changing the graph."
  approach: "Discover iteration folders -> let user review -> sample status/query/convergence -> let user review -> emit recommendation."
  mandate: "This command is read-only by contract."

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# USER INPUTS
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
user_inputs:
  execution_mode: "[EXECUTION_MODE] - INTERACTIVE (this YAML)"
  intent: "[INTENT] - DIAGNOSE (this YAML)"
  scope: "[SCOPE] - research|review|both; default both"

# Prepare the system-spec-kit runtime before /doctor:update asks OpenCode for MCP tools.
set -euo pipefail

ROOT=""
JSON_MODE=false

while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
  case "$1" in
    --root)
      if [[ $# -lt 2 ]]; then
        echo "doctor-runtime-bootstrap: --root requires a path" >&2
        exit 2
      fi
      ROOT="$2"
      shift 2
      ;;
    --json)
      JSON_MODE=true
      shift

display.text:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Prepare the system-spec-kit runtime before /doctor:update asks OpenCode for MCP tools.
set -euo pipefail

ROOT=""
JSON_MODE=false

while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
  case "$1" in
    --root)
      if [[ $# -lt 2 ]]; then
        echo "doctor-runtime-bootstrap: --root requires a path" >&2
        exit 2
      fi
      ROOT="$2"
      shift 2
      ;;
    --json)
      JSON_MODE=true
      shift
      ;;
    --help|-h)
      cat <<'HELP'
Usage: bash .opencode/commands/doctor/scripts/doctor-runtime-bootstrap.sh [--root <workspace>] [--json]

Creates the legacy .opencode/skill -> .opencode/skills bridge when needed,
installs system-spec-kit workspace dependencies, and builds the MCP server and
script runtimes needed by /doctor:update.
HELP
      exit 0
      ;;
    *)
      echo "doctor-runtime-bootstrap: unknown option: $1" >&2
      exit 2
      ;;
  esac
done

if [[ -z "$ROOT" ]]; then
  ROOT="$(pwd)"
fi
ROOT="$(cd "$ROOT" && pwd)"

OPENCODE_DIR="$ROOT/.opencode"
SKILLS_DIR="$OPENCODE_DIR/skills"
LEGACY_SKILL_DIR="$OPENCODE_DIR/skill"
KIT_DIR="$SKILLS_DIR/system-spec-kit"
DB_DIR="$KIT_DIR/mcp_server/database"
STATE_FILE="$DB_DIR/.doctor-update.bootstrap.json"
LOCK_FILE="/tmp/doctor-runtime-bootstrap.lock"
MCP_DIST="$KIT_DIR/mcp_server/dist/context-server.js"
GRAPH_BACKFILL_DIST="$KIT_DIR/scripts/dist/graph/backfill-graph-metadata.js"
DESCRIPTION_DIST="$KIT_DIR/scripts/dist/spec-folder/generate-description.js"

started_at="$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)"
actions_file="$(mktemp "${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/doctor-runtime-bootstrap-actions.XXXXXX")"
restart_required=false

finish_state() {

153: function safeMany(values: readonly string[] | undefined): string[] {
154:   return (values ?? [])
155:     .map((value) => sanitizeSkillLabel(value))
156:     .filter((value): value is string => Boolean(value));
157: }
158: 
159: function publicRecommendation(recommendation: ScoredRecommendation, includeAttribution: boolean) {
160:   const skillId = sanitizeSkillLabel(recommendation.skill);
161:   if (!skillId) return null;
162:   const redirectFrom = safeMany(recommendation.redirectFrom);
163:   const redirectTo = sanitizeSkillLabel(recommendation.redirectTo ?? '');
164:   const sanitizedStatus = sanitizeSkillLabel(recommendation.lifecycleStatus);
165:   const status: PublicRecommendationStatus | undefined = sanitizedStatus === 'active'
166:     || sanitizedStatus === 'deprecated'
167:     || sanitizedStatus === 'archived'
168:     || sanitizedStatus === 'future'
169:     ? sanitizedStatus as PublicRecommendationStatus
170:     : undefined;
171:   return {
172:     skillId,
173:     score: recommendation.score,
174:     confidence: recommendation.confidence,
175:     uncertainty: recommendation.uncertainty,
176:     dominantLane: recommendation.dominantLane,
177:     ...(includeAttribution ? {
178:       laneBreakdown: recommendation.laneContributions.map((lane) => ({
179:         lane: lane.lane,
180:         rawScore: lane.rawScore,
181:         weightedScore: lane.weightedScore,
182:         weight: lane.weight,
183:         shadowOnly: lane.shadowOnly,
184:       })),
185:     } : {}),
186:     ...(redirectFrom.length > 0 ? { redirectFrom } : {}),
187:     ...(redirectTo ? { redirectTo } : {}),
188:     ...(status ? { status } : {}),
189:   };
190: }
191: 
192: function publicShadowRecommendations(recommendations: readonly ScoredRecommendation[], topK: number): ShadowRecommendation[] {
193:   const shadowRecommendations: ShadowRecommendation[] = [];
194:   for (const recommendation of recommendations) {
195:     const skillId = sanitizeSkillLabel(recommendation.skill);
196:     if (!skillId) continue;
197:     const shadowContributions = recommendation.laneContributions.map((lane) => ({
198:       lane: lane.lane,
199:       weightedScore: lane.rawScore * DEFAULT_SHADOW_SCORER_LANE_WEIGHTS[lane.lane],
200:     }));
201:     const shadowScore = roundScore(shadowContributions.reduce((total, lane) => total + lane.weightedScore, 0));
202:     const dominant = shadowContributions
203:       .filter((lane) => lane.weightedScore > 0)
204:       .sort((left, right) => right.weightedScore - left.weightedScore)[0]?.lane ?? null;
205:     shadowRecommendations.push({
206:         skillId,
207:         liveScore: recommendation.score,
208:         shadowScore,
209:         delta: roundScore(shadowScore - recommendation.score),
210:         dominantShadowLane: dominant,
211:     });
212:   }
213:   return shadowRecommendations
214:     .sort((left, right) => right.shadowScore - left.shadowScore || left.skillId.localeCompare(right.skillId))
215:     .slice(0, topK);
216: }
217: 
218: function roundScore(value: number): number {
219:   return Math.round(value * 1_000_000) / 1_000_000;
220: }
221: 
222: async function computeRecommendationOutput(input: AdvisorRecommendInput): Promise<AdvisorRecommendOutput> {
223:   // Canonicalize via realpath after the schema allowlist check.
224:   const workspaceRoot = input.workspaceRoot
225:     ? canonicalizeWorkspaceRoot(input.workspaceRoot)
226:     : findWorkspaceRoot();
227:   const effectiveThresholds = publicThresholds({
228:     confidenceThreshold: input.options?.confidenceThreshold,
229:     uncertaintyThreshold: input.options?.uncertaintyThreshold,
230:   });
231:   const status = readAdvisorStatus({ workspaceRoot });
232:   if (status.freshness === 'unavailable') {
233:     return unavailableOutput(status, workspaceRoot, effectiveThresholds);
234:   }
235:   if (status.freshness === 'absent') {
236:     return absentOutput(status, workspaceRoot, effectiveThresholds);
237:   }
238:   const sourceSignature = cacheSourceSignature(status);
239:   advisorPromptCache.invalidateSourceSignatureChange(sourceSignature);
240:   const key = advisorPromptCache.makeKey({
241:     canonicalPrompt: input.prompt.trim(),
242:     sourceSignature,
243:     workspaceRoot,
244:     runtime: 'mcp',
245:     maxTokens: input.options?.topK,
246:     thresholdConfig: {
247:       confidenceThreshold: input.options?.confidenceThreshold,
248:       uncertaintyThreshold: input.options?.uncertaintyThreshold,
249:       includeAttribution: input.options?.includeAttribution,
250:       includeAbstainReasons: input.options?.includeAbstainReasons,
251:     },
252:   });
253:   const cached = advisorPromptCache.get(key);
254:   if (cached) {
255:     const cachedOutput = cached.value as AdvisorRecommendOutput;
256:     return AdvisorRecommendOutputSchema.parse({
257:       ...cachedOutput,
258:       workspaceRoot,
259:       effectiveThresholds,
260:       cache: {
261:         ...cachedOutput.cache,
262:         hit: true,
263:       },
264:     });
265:   }
266: 
267:   const topK = input.options?.topK ?? 3;
268:   const result = await withSemanticShadowPromptEmbedding(input.prompt, () => scoreAdvisorPrompt(input.prompt, {
269:     workspaceRoot,
270:     confidenceThreshold: input.options?.confidenceThreshold,
271:     uncertaintyThreshold: input.options?.uncertaintyThreshold,
272:   }));
273:   const recommendations = result.recommendations
274:     .map((recommendation) => publicRecommendation(recommendation, Boolean(input.options?.includeAttribution)))
275:     .filter((recommendation): recommendation is NonNullable<typeof recommendation> => Boolean(recommendation))
276:     .slice(0, topK);
277:   const output: AdvisorRecommendOutput = {
278:     workspaceRoot,
279:     effectiveThresholds,
280:     recommendations,
281:     ambiguous: result.ambiguous,
282:     freshness: status.freshness,
283:     trustState: status.trustState,
284:     generatedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
285:     cache: {
286:       hit: false,
287:       sourceSignaturePresent: status.generation > 0 || status.lastGenerationBump !== null,
288:     },
289:     _shadow: {

291:       liveWeightsFrozen: true,
292:       recommendations: publicShadowRecommendations(result.recommendations, topK),
293:     },

298:   };
299:   const parsed = AdvisorRecommendOutputSchema.parse(output);

302:       prompt: input.prompt,
303:       recommendation: shadow.skillId,
304:       liveScore: shadow.liveScore,
305:       shadowScore: shadow.shadowScore,
306:       delta: shadow.delta,
307:       dominantLane: shadow.dominantShadowLane,
308:       timestamp: parsed.generatedAt,
309:     });
310:   }
311:   advisorPromptCache.set({
312:     key,
313:     sourceSignature,
314:     value: parsed,
315:     skillLabels: recommendationLabels(parsed),
316:   });
317:   return parsed;
318: }

337:     }],
338:   };
339: }

Three blocking guards.","evidence":"convergence.cjs:262","relevance":0.9,"notes":"Reuse catalog coverage is weighted highest (0.30) per reuse-first principle. Returns 0-1 rounded to 3 decimals.","evidence":"convergence.cjs:116","relevance":0.85,"notes":"Loop-type-specific weight tables; context weights reuseCatalog highest at 0.30. Used by convergence entrypoint to surface score alongside blockers."},{"path":".opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/upsert.cjs","symbol":"acquireWriterLock","kind":"convention","signature":"function acquireWriterLock(lockPath): () => void (release)","use":"Exclusive `wx` file-open writer lock with retry-on-contention. Returns release function. Defense-in-depth against path traversal in namespace-scoped values."},{"path":".opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/lib/cli-guards.cjs","symbol":"classifyExitCode","kind":"convention","signature":"function classifyExitCode(err): number","use":"Maps error.code to exit code: INPUT_VALIDATION→3, DB/SQLITE/EACCES→2, default→1. Every CLI entrypoint follows this contract."},{"path":".opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-run.cjs","symbol":"buildLineageCommand","kind":"reuse_candidate","signature":"function buildLineageCommand(lineage, prompt, resolvedSandbox, resolvedPermission): { command, args, input?, env }","use":"Builds the CLI subprocess command for a fan-out lineage. Timeout via SIGTERM; maxBuffer cap at 20MB.","evidence":"fanout-run.cjs:188","relevance":0.9,"notes":"Key design: returns Promise so the pool's K-in-flight cap actually overlaps. Drains stderr so child never blocks on full pipe. SIGTERM on timeout; signal field preserves timeout-as-failure signal."},{"path":".opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-salvage.cjs","symbol":"runSalvageSweep","kind":"reuse_candidate","signature":"function runSalvageSweep(lineageDir, loopType, savedStdout): { salvaged, failed }","use":"Recovers missing iteration files from captured subprocess stdout after a CLI executor fails to write outputs. Writes salvaged_from_stdout events to state log.","evidence":"fanout-salvage.cjs:69","relevance":0.85,"notes":"Runs before the failure throw so iteration recovery is never lost. extractTextFromOpencodeJson parses opencode --format json text parts from stdout."},{"path":".opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-merge.cjs","symbol":"mergeResearchRegistries","kind":"reuse_candidate","signature":"function mergeResearchRegistries(lineageData): mergedRegistry","use":"Deduplicates research findings by findingId across lineages; merges open/resolved questions and ruled-out directions with _lineages attribution.","evidence":"fanout-merge.cjs:91","relevance":0.85,"notes":"Cross-model attribution via lineage labels. Cross-lineage P0 wins. Verdict: FAIL if any P0, CONDITIONAL if any P1, else PASS. Exported for unit testing."},{"path":".opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/lib/council/multi-seat-dispatch.cjs","symbol":"dispatchCouncilSeats","kind":"reuse_candidate","signature":"async function dispatchCouncilSeats({ roundId, seats, dispatchSeat, context?, now? }): Promise<{ round_id, started_at_iso, completed_at_iso, results[], summary }>","use":"Parallel council seat dispatcher. Probes daemon socket (deepProbe=true requires JSON-RPC reply), bridges stdio to socket if alive, returns respawn decision if dead.","evidence":"launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:311","relevance":0.95,"notes":"deepProbe=true detects hung daemon (accepts connection but never responds).
