Used by all 3 launcher types."},{"path":".opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs","symbol":"probeDaemon","kind":"integration_point","signature":"function probeDaemon(socketPath, options?): Promise<{ status, reason }>","use":"Liveness probe for MCP daemon sockets. Sends JSON-RPC initialize request when deepProbe=true. Returns 'alive' or 'dead' with reason (timeout, socket-error, json-rpc-reply, etc).","evidence":"launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:124","relevance":0.9,"notes":"Uses StringDecoder for multibyte UTF-8 safety across chunk boundaries. Bounded probe timeout (default 5s, max 6999ms). The reap/bridge decision always passes deepProbe:true."},{"path":".opencode/bin/lib/launcher-session-proxy.cjs","symbol":"createSessionProxy","kind":"integration_point","signature":"function createSessionProxy({ socketPath, stdin?, stdout?, probe?, connect?, log?, classify?, maxReattachAttempts?, maxColdStartAttempts?, keepaliveIntervalMs? }): { start, stop }","use":"Reconnecting MCP session proxy. Survives daemon recycles transparently by re-attaching to fresh sockets and replaying pending requests. Classifies frames as replayable vs unsafe.","evidence":"launcher-session-proxy.cjs:338","relevance":0.95,"notes":"Three states: REATTACHING → CONNECTED → CLOSED. Deep-probe wait with backoff ladder [100,250,500,1000,1500]ms. Pending request tracker distinguishes replayable (memory_search, session_resume, etc) from unsafe (memory_delete, checkpoint_restore). Keepalive pings every 10s with 5s timeout. Protocol version mismatch is terminal (non-retryable -32002)."},{"path":".opencode/bin/lib/launcher-session-proxy.cjs","symbol":"classifyFrame","kind":"convention","signature":"function classifyFrame(frame): boolean","use":"Determines if a JSON-RPC frame is safe to replay after daemon recycle. REPLAYABLE_TOOL_NAMES (memory_search, session_resume, etc) return true; UNSAFE_TOOL_NAMES (memory_delete, checkpoint_restore) return false.","evidence":"launcher-session-proxy.cjs:123","relevance":0.9,"notes":"initialize and ping are always replayable. notifications/ methods are replayable. tools/call checks tool name against two sets. memory_save is replayable due to content-hash dedup."},{"path":".opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs","symbol":"acquireOwnerLeaseFile","kind":"integration_point","signature":"function acquireOwnerLeaseFile(): { acquired, lease?, holder?, classification, reclaimed? }","use":"Single-writer ownership via exclusive fs.openSync('wx') on owner lease file. Classifies existing lease: stale-pid, ppid-1-orphan, stale-heartbeat-reclaim, live-owner, unknown-eperm.","evidence":"mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:365","relevance":0.95,"notes":"Heartbeat TTL: 60s, refreshed at TTL/2 interval. Re-read after atomic rename confirms surviving owner. classifyOwnerLease checks liveness + ppid + heartbeat. writeOwnerLeaseFileExclusive uses wx+fsync for crash durability."},{"path":".opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs","symbol":"recycleDaemonInPlace","kind":"integration_point","signature":"async function recycleDaemonInPlace(graceMs, deps?): Promise<void>","use":"RSS-breach-triggered in-place daemon recycle. Sends SIGTERM then SIGKILL to model-server and context-server, but keeps the launcher lease held across recycle so replacement daemon is respawned by child exit handler.","evidence":"mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:1053","relevance":0.9,"notes":"Deps are injectable for testing (getContextChild, hfControl, isChildRunning, etc). Key design: lease is NOT cleared during recycle — launcher process stays alive, replacement daemon respawns via backoff. rssBreachSelfExitInProgress flag prevents re-entrant recycle."},{"path":".opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs","symbol":"writeAtomicJsonFile","kind":"convention","signature":"function writeAtomicJsonFile(targetPath, payload, operation): boolean","use":"Atomic JSON file write via tmp+rename pattern. PID-suffixed temp file, 0o600 mode. Handles durable-write-unavailable codes (ENOSPC, EDQUOT, EEROFS) gracefully.","evidence":"mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:140","relevance":0.9,"notes":"Convention across launcher subsystem: write tmp → rename atomic. Durable-write failures are logged once and return false (best-effort) rather than crashing. Used for lease files, pid files, state files."},{"path":".opencode/bin/lib/model-server-supervision.cjs","symbol":"createModelServerSupervisor","kind":"reuse_candidate","signature":"function createModelServerSupervisor(options?): ModelServerSupervisor","use":"Shared hf-model-server launcher supervision. The launcher-local factory pre-seeds writeLease/shouldExitLauncher/onRssBreach defaults."},{"path":".opencode/commands/doctor/assets/doctor_update.yaml","symbol":"/doctor:update orchestrator","kind":"integration_point","signature":"YAML workflow: flock → probe → migrate → snapshot → dashboard → dependency.order → validation → state.log","use":"Single-instance, flock-protected database rebuild chain. Restores failed+downstream DBs on retry exhaustion.","evidence":"doctor_update.yaml:24","relevance":0.9,"notes":"SIGINT follows ADR-001/ADR-007: set cancel, ~5s SQLite settle window, restore in-flight snapshot, log, exit 130. Every abort path must rm -f .doctor-update.flock before exit. Reads memory_health + memory_stats → classifies drift signals → emits recommendations. Never mutates spec docs or skill files.","evidence":"doctor_memory.yaml:4","relevance":0.85,"notes":"Mutation boundaries explicitly listed: allowed_targets are provider-keyed sqlite DBs only. Forbidden: spec docs, skill files, code-graph DBs, deep-loop DBs. Applies ambiguity detection, breadth/multi-concern abstention gates.","evidence":"fusion.ts:1","relevance":0.95,"notes":"Lane weights overridable via SPECKIT_ADVISOR_LANE_WEIGHTS_JSON env. Shadow scoring with learned weights runs alongside live scoring for delta telemetry."},{"path":".opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/lib/scorer/lane-registry.ts","symbol":"SCORER_LANE_REGISTRY","kind":"convention","signature":"const SCORER_LANE_REGISTRY: readonly { id, weight, shadowWeight, live, damping? }[]","use":"Canonical lane definitions with live/shadow weights. 5 lanes: explicit_author, lexical, graph_causal, derived_generated, semantic_shadow.
